raw_consumer_statement:
To Whom It May Concern, I am writing regarding my American Express business credit card, account number XXXX, with a balance of {$670.00}. I have previously received a letter from American Express dated XX/XX/2024, acknowledging that a computer error occurred and confirming that I did not owe any balance on my credit card. The letter indicated that the necessary corrections would be made to resolve this issue. 

Despite this acknowledgment, my credit card has been closed, and derogatory marks related to this account remain on my credit report. This is deeply concerning as these errors are the result of a technical issue on American Expresss side, not due to any fault of mine. The closure and negative reporting have significantly impacted my business operations, as I relied on this credit card for essential business expenses. 

The situation arose after my original credit card was stolen, which prompted my request for a replacement. I do not understand why my account has been closed instead of being properly corrected as promised. I respectfully request the following actions be taken immediately : 1. Reinstate my business credit card account and issue a new credit card to replace the stolen one.

2. Remove all derogatory marks associated with this account from my credit report, ensuring that no negative impact remains due to the technical error. 


XXXX. Provide written confirmation that these corrections have been made and that my account is in good standing. 



I have always valued my relationship with American Express and hope to resolve this matter promptly. Thank you for your attention to this issue, and I look forward to a swift resolution that allows me to continue using my business credit card without further disruptions. 

Sincerely,
